# sysdesign

> A Claude Code plugin that gives an AI coding agent system-design knowledge: one skill,
> ten commands, and fourteen self-contained reference files. The method is consistent —
> state the constraint, pick the option that fits it, name what you gave up. Original prose,
> no external links, MIT-licensed. Inspired by the taxonomy of ByteByteGo's System Design 101.

This site is a single-page landing built with Astro, hosted at https://sysdesign.mkabumattar.com.

## Install (in Claude Code)

- `/plugin marketplace add mkabumattar/sysdesign`
- `/plugin install sysdesign@sysdesign`

## Commands

- explain — what a concept is, when to use it, the tradeoff
- compare — options compared, one recommended for a stated constraint
- review — pressure-test an architecture for SPOFs and missing safeguards
- choose — pick a database / queue / cache / deployment under constraints
- estimate — back-of-envelope QPS, storage, bandwidth, memory
- tradeoffs — name what a design choice gains and gives up
- diagram — sketch an architecture as Mermaid or ASCII
- interview — the 7-step system-design interview framework
- cheatsheet — condense an area into a scannable sheet
- help — list commands and reference topics

## Reference topics

api-web, data-storage, caching-performance, distributed-systems, security-auth, devops-k8s,
architecture-patterns, case-studies, networking, os-concurrency, payments, ai-ml-systems,
dev-tools, interview.
